title: Algebra 1: Getting Started and Building a Strong Foundation
lead: Algebra 1 is a turning point. The shift from arithmetic to variables can trip students up. Here's how to set a solid foundation.

sections.title: The Jump from Numbers to Letters
content: Variables represent unknowns. For many kids, that abstract step is the first real challenge. Start with simple substitutions: if x = 5, what's 2x? Building that bridge from concrete numbers to letters helps.

sections.title: Core Skills to Master Early
content: Order of operations, combining like terms, solving one-step and two-step equations. These come up in every algebra topic. Nail them first.

sections.title: Common Stumbling Blocks
content: Negative numbers, fractions in equations, and word problems. These trip students even when they understand the mechanics. Extra practice on these areas pays off.

sections.title: When to Get Help
content: If your child is falling behind in the first few chapters, don't wait. Algebra builds on itself. Early intervention prevents bigger gaps later.

cta.title: Strengthen Algebra Skills
cta.text: GuruForU math tutors specialize in algebra and can help your child build confidence.

faq.question: What is the biggest jump when starting Algebra 1?
faq.answer: Moving from numbers to variables. Start with simple substitutions (if x = 5, what's 2x?) to build the bridge from concrete numbers to letters.

faq.question: What core skills should students master first in algebra?
faq.answer: Order of operations, combining like terms, solving one-step and two-step equations. These come up in every algebra topic.

faq.question: What are common stumbling blocks in algebra?
faq.answer: Negative numbers, fractions in equations, and word problems. Extra practice on these areas pays off even when students understand the mechanics.

faq.question: When should I get help for algebra?
faq.answer: If your child is falling behind in the first few chapters, don't wait. Algebra builds on itself, and early intervention prevents bigger gaps later.
